title: dalea lagopus
intro: Dalea lagopus, also known as the white prairie clover, is a perennial legume native to North America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for treating wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Dalea lagopus include its ability to lower blood pressure, improve cardiovascular health, and provide antioxidant properties. The herbal preparations of Dalea lagopus typically involve making a tea from the dried flowers and leaves, or using it as a tincture, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
